title: pterocarpus dubius
intro: Pterocarpus dubius is a plant species in the Fabaceae family, commonly known as the yellow mukul or mukul tree. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic medicine, and is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Pterocarpus dubius include the ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from digestive problems, among others. The plant is often prepared as an herbal infusion, decoction, or oil extraction, which can be used to treat various health conditions or as a preventative measure to maintain overall well-being.
